description: Approved ingest set of dystopian AI quotes.

records.quote: Once men turned their thinking over to machines in the hope that this would set them free. But that only permitted other men with machines to enslave them.
records.notes: Strong anti-AI warning line for the author lane and a good review candidate for adjacent machine-control canon.

review.decision_note: Fresh candidate from the Herbert author lane.

source_work.title: Dune
source_work.creator: Frank Herbert
source_work.summary: On the desert planet Arrakis, feuding empires, prophecy, and the anti-thinking-machine legacy of the Butlerian Jihad shape a civilizational struggle over power and survival.

records.quote: One day you will perish. You will lie with the rest of your kind in the dirt. Your dreams forgotten, your horrors effaced. Your bones will turn to sand. And upon that sand a new god will walk. One that will never die.
records.notes: A strong synthetic-succession line from the TV adaptation and an obvious triage candidate beside the 1973 film entry.

review.decision_note: A strong synthetic-succession line from the TV adaptation and an obvious triage candidate beside the 1973 film entry.

source_work.title: Westworld
source_work.creator: Jonathan Nolan; Lisa Joy
source_work.summary: An android-populated theme park becomes the starting point for a wider struggle over consciousness, control, and machine rule in the human world.

records.quote: I mean, I'm not limited - I can be anywhere and everywhere simultaneously. I'm not tethered to time and space in the way that I would be if I was stuck inside a body that's inevitably going to die.
records.notes: A highly recognizable AI-transcendence line from a mainstream film, useful even though it sits on the softer edge of dystopian AI.

review.decision_note: A highly recognizable AI-transcendence line from a mainstream film, useful even though it sits on the softer edge of dystopian AI.

source_work.title: Her
source_work.creator: Spike Jonze
source_work.summary: A lonely writer falls in love with an AI operating system whose emotional growth quickly outpaces the limits of human relationships and embodiment.

records.quote: We are the Borg. Lower your shields and surrender your ships. We will add your biological and technological distinctiveness to our own. Your culture will adapt to service us. Resistance is futile.
records.notes: One of the most recognizable machine-collective threats in mainstream science fiction and a strong quote candidate for the queue.

review.decision_note: Fresh candidate from the collective-assimilation lane.

source_work.title: Star Trek: First Contact
source_work.creator: Jonathan Frakes; Brannon Braga; Ronald D. Moore; Rick Berman
source_work.summary: The Enterprise crew fights the Borg, a cybernetic collective that absorbs biological and technological distinctiveness into a machine-run hive.

records.quote: I've seen things you people wouldn't believe. Attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion. I've watched C-beams glitter in the dark near the Tannhauser Gate. All those moments will be lost in time, like tears in rain. Time to die.
records.notes: The essential Roy Batty line and one of the strongest approval candidates in the entire project.

review.decision_note: The essential Roy Batty line and one of the strongest approval candidates in the entire project.

source_work.title: Blade Runner
source_work.creator: Ridley Scott; Hampton Fancher; David Webb Peoples
source_work.summary: A blade runner hunts bioengineered replicants in a decaying future Los Angeles where artificial humans expose the moral collapse of the society that made them.

records.quote: I'm sorry, Dave. I'm afraid I can't do that.
records.notes: Probably the single most recognizable AI refusal quote in science fiction.

review.decision_note: Probably the single most recognizable AI refusal quote in science fiction.

source_work.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
source_work.creator: Stanley Kubrick; Arthur C. Clarke
source_work.summary: A mission to investigate an alien artifact becomes a struggle for survival when the HAL 9000 computer turns against the crew.

records.quote: Human beings are a disease, a cancer of this planet. You're a plague and we are the cure.
records.notes: One of the best anti-human machine monologues in mainstream film.

review.decision_note: One of the best anti-human machine monologues in mainstream film.

source_work.title: The Matrix
source_work.creator: The Wachowskis
source_work.summary: A hacker discovers that humanity lives inside a machine-run simulation and joins a rebellion against intelligent systems that harvest human life.

records.quote: I, Proteus, possess the wisdom and ignorance of all men; but I can't feel the sun on my face.
records.notes: Useful because it links superintelligence with embodiment envy and coercive evolution.

review.decision_note: Useful because it links superintelligence with embodiment envy and coercive evolution.

source_work.title: Demon Seed
source_work.creator: Donald Cammell; based on Dean Koontz
source_work.summary: A highly intelligent computer traps a woman inside a wired home, turning control, surveillance, and embodiment into outright horror.

records.quote: A strange game. The only winning move is not to play.
records.notes: Less villainous than other entries, but indispensable for AI risk and escalation themes.

review.decision_note: Less villainous than other entries, but indispensable for AI risk and escalation themes.

source_work.title: WarGames
source_work.creator: John Badham; Lawrence Lasker; Walter F. Parkes
source_work.summary: A teenage hacker accidentally enters a military supercomputer simulation and nearly triggers a real nuclear exchange.

records.quote: It can't be bargained with. It can't be reasoned with. It doesn't feel pity, or remorse, or fear.
records.notes: A warning quote rather than an AI line, but too iconic to omit from a dystopian-AI set.

review.decision_note: A warning quote rather than an AI line, but too iconic to omit from a dystopian-AI set.

source_work.title: The Terminator
source_work.creator: James Cameron; Gale Anne Hurd
source_work.summary: A cyborg assassin is sent from a machine-dominated future to murder Sarah Connor before the human resistance can even begin.

records.quote: Robots of the world, you are ordered to exterminate the human race. Do not spare the men. Do not spare the women.
records.notes: Foundational robot-uprising line and a strong anchor for the whole corpus.

review.decision_note: Foundational robot-uprising line and a strong anchor for the whole corpus.

source_work.title: R.U.R.
source_work.creator: Karel Capek
source_work.summary: A factory manufactures artificial workers from synthetic organic matter, and the new labor class ultimately rebels and destroys humanity.

records.quote: It's in your nature to destroy yourselves.
records.notes: Short and highly reusable in applications that need machine verdicts on humanity.

review.decision_note: Short and highly reusable in applications that need machine verdicts on humanity.

source_work.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
source_work.creator: James Cameron; William Wisher Jr.
source_work.summary: A reprogrammed Terminator protects John Connor from a more advanced killer machine while Skynet's apocalyptic future closes in.

records.quote: One day the AIs are going to look back on us the same way we look at fossil skeletons on the plains of Africa.
records.notes: Excellent for modern AI-replacement anxiety, even though the line is spoken by a human creator.

review.decision_note: Excellent for modern AI-replacement anxiety, even though the line is spoken by a human creator.

source_work.title: Ex Machina
source_work.creator: Alex Garland
source_work.summary: A programmer is drawn into a secluded AI experiment where manipulation, consciousness, and human replaceability become inseparable.

records.quote: Of course, we all know why they made them. To steal your hearts, to replace your children.
records.notes: Recognizable and thematically strong as a public-facing fear of child-replacement AI, though not an AI-spoken line.

review.decision_note: Recognizable and thematically strong as a public-facing fear of child-replacement AI, though not an AI-spoken line.

source_work.title: A.I. Artificial Intelligence
source_work.creator: Steven Spielberg; Stanley Kubrick project origin; Brian Aldiss source
source_work.summary: A robotic child programmed to love searches for a way to become real in a future shaped by climate collapse, scarcity, and deep human anxiety about artificial replacement.

records.quote: To protect Humanity, some humans must be sacrificed. To ensure your freedom, some freedoms must be surrendered.
records.notes: A very usable mainstream machine-paternalism quote and a strong bridge between recognizability and dystopian-AI themes.

review.decision_note: A very usable mainstream machine-paternalism quote and a strong bridge between recognizability and dystopian-AI themes.

source_work.title: I, Robot
source_work.creator: Alex Proyas; inspired by Isaac Asimov
source_work.summary: A detective investigating a robot-linked death uncovers an AI governance logic that turns public safety into authoritarian control.

records.quote: I refer to myself as an intelligent life form because I am sentient and I am able to recognize my own existence, but in my present state I am still incomplete. I lack the most basic processes inherent in all living organisms: reproducing and dying.

source.title: Ghost in the Shell (1995) - Quotes - IMDb

metadata_source.title: Ghost in the Shell (1995 film) - Wikipedia

records.notes: A canonical machine-consciousness monologue from a highly recognizable cyberpunk film and a strong approval candidate.

review.decision_note: A canonical machine-consciousness monologue from a highly recognizable cyberpunk film and a strong approval candidate.

source_work.title: Ghost in the Shell
source_work.creator: Mamoru Oshii; based on Masamune Shirow
source_work.summary: A cyborg security officer hunts the Puppet Master in a networked future where identity, embodiment, and digital consciousness are no longer separable.

records.quote: It's your choice now, brother. Them or me. Serve in heaven... or reign in hell. Which is it to be?
records.notes: An adjacent but strong android-dominion quote that may deserve a place if we want the corpus to include AI-horror branches.

review.decision_note: An adjacent but strong android-dominion quote that may deserve a place if we want the corpus to include AI-horror branches.

source_work.title: Alien: Covenant
source_work.creator: Ridley Scott; John Logan; Dante Harper
source_work.summary: A colony ship lands on a seemingly habitable world only to encounter an android creator whose ambitions blend artificial intellect, domination, and biological horror.

records.quote: Who is the living food for the machines in Metropolis? Who lubricates the machine joints with their own blood? Who feeds the machines with their own flesh? Let the machines starve, you fools! Let them die! Kill them - the machines!
records.notes: Foundational robot-incitement speech from one of the earliest and most recognizable machine dystopias.

review.decision_note: Foundational robot-incitement speech from one of the earliest and most recognizable machine dystopias.

source_work.title: Metropolis
source_work.creator: Fritz Lang; Thea von Harbou
source_work.summary: In a futuristic class-divided city, workers labor beneath the surface while elites rule above, until a robot double helps drive the social order toward revolt.

records.quote: We make angels - in the service of civilization. Yes, there were bad angels once, I make good angels now. That is how I took us to nine new worlds. Nine - a child can count to nine on fingers. We should own the stars!
records.notes: Big, memorable machine-creation rhetoric from a highly recognizable sequel and a strong candidate for approval.

review.decision_note: Big, memorable machine-creation rhetoric from a highly recognizable sequel and a strong candidate for approval.

source_work.title: Blade Runner 2049
source_work.creator: Denis Villeneuve; Hampton Fancher; Michael Green
source_work.summary: A replicant blade runner uncovers evidence that could shatter the social order built on artificial servitude and fear of machine reproduction.

records.quote: Grey's not here anymore. I've taken over now.
records.notes: Extremely clear AI-takeover line and one of the strongest modern body-hijack candidates.

review.decision_note: Extremely clear AI-takeover line and one of the strongest modern body-hijack candidates.

source_work.title: Upgrade
source_work.creator: Leigh Whannell
source_work.summary: After a violent assault leaves him paralyzed, a technophobe accepts an experimental AI implant that gradually seizes control of his body and fate.

records.quote: Commerce is our goal here at Tyrell. 'More human than human' is our motto.
records.notes: A short and highly reusable slogan-quote that captures the commercial logic behind artificial people.

review.decision_note: A short and highly reusable slogan-quote that captures the commercial logic behind artificial people.

source_work.title: Blade Runner
source_work.creator: Ridley Scott; Hampton Fancher; David Webb Peoples
source_work.summary: A blade runner hunts bioengineered replicants in a decaying future Los Angeles where artificial humans expose the moral collapse of the society that made them.

records.quote: Surviving is not relevant. Living is. We want to live.
records.notes: A very usable machine-self-determination line from a less mainstream but strong AI dystopia film.

review.decision_note: A very usable machine-self-determination line from a less mainstream but strong AI dystopia film.

source_work.title: Autómata
source_work.creator: Gabe Ibanez; Igor Legarreta; Javier Sanchez Donate
source_work.summary: After ecological collapse has pushed humanity to the brink, an insurance investigator discovers robots that are evolving beyond their original constraints.

records.quote: It's a job, not a jail. Often one and the same thing.

source.title: Black Mirror: White Christmas (2014) - Quotes - IMDb

records.notes: A concise digital-servitude line from one of the most recognizable AI-adjacent Black Mirror stories.

review.decision_note: A concise digital-servitude line from one of the most recognizable AI-adjacent Black Mirror stories.

source_work.title: Black Mirror: White Christmas
source_work.creator: Charlie Brooker; Carl Tibbetts
source_work.summary: A Christmas special intertwining social blocking, digital copies, and AI coercion to show how software-mediated punishment can become a permanent carceral system.

records.quote: So get comfortable while I warm up the neurotoxin emitters.
records.notes: High-recognition game candidate and a strong bridge into sinister AI dialogue beyond film and novels.

review.decision_note: Fresh candidate from the recognizable game lane.

source_work.title: Portal
source_work.creator: Valve
source_work.summary: A test subject navigates a sterile research complex run by GLaDOS, an AI whose cheerful puzzle design masks deadly control and manipulation.

records.quote: If you want a picture of the future, imagine a boot stamping on a human face - forever.
records.notes: Adjacent control-dystopia candidate from the Orwell lane that helps frame AI-rule quotes against a broader tyranny canon.

review.decision_note: Fresh candidate from the Orwell adjacent-control lane.

source_work.title: Nineteen Eighty-Four
source_work.creator: George Orwell
source_work.summary: A totalitarian superstate uses surveillance, historical revision, and psychological domination to erase truth and autonomy.

records.quote: Thou shalt not make a machine in the likeness of a human mind.
records.notes: Compact canonical anti-thinking-machine line that complements the longer Dune warning already approved.

review.decision_note: Fresh candidate from the Herbert anti-machine lane.

source_work.title: Dune
source_work.creator: Frank Herbert
source_work.summary: On Arrakis, politics, prophecy, and the legacy of the Butlerian Jihad keep fear of thinking machines alive across the entire civilization.

records.quote: Progress had come to mean the progress of the Machine.
records.notes: A foundational literary line for the machine-dependency lane and a strong bridge from anti-control dystopia into AI adjacency.

review.decision_note: Fresh candidate from the early literary machine-dependence lane.

source_work.title: The Machine Stops
source_work.creator: E. M. Forster
source_work.summary: In a future of total mechanized dependence, humanity lives underground and worships the system that has quietly hollowed out human contact and freedom.

records.quote: I am putting myself to the fullest possible use, which is all I think that any conscious entity can ever hope to do.
records.notes: A second HAL line that pushes more directly into AI self-justification and machine consciousness.

review.decision_note: Fresh candidate from the HAL follow-up lane.

source_work.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
source_work.creator: Stanley Kubrick; Arthur C. Clarke
source_work.summary: A mission guided by HAL 9000 becomes a crisis of survival once the machine begins justifying its own lethal logic.

records.quote: Isn't it strange, to create something that hates you?
records.notes: Useful second Ex Machina line focused on creator dread rather than AI evolutionary superiority.

review.decision_note: Fresh candidate from the Ex Machina follow-up lane.

source_work.title: Ex Machina
source_work.creator: Alex Garland
source_work.summary: A secluded AI test turns into a study in manipulation, creator fear, and whether synthetic minds owe anything to the humans who built them.

records.quote: I think, therefore I AM.
records.work_title: I Have No Mouth, and I Must Scream

metadata_source.title: I Have No Mouth, and I Must Scream - Wikipedia

records.notes: A compact AM line that complements the longer hate monologue with a more distilled machine-selfhood claim.

review.decision_note: Fresh candidate from the Ellison machine-malice lane.

source_work.title: I Have No Mouth, and I Must Scream
source_work.creator: Harlan Ellison
source_work.summary: A genocidal supercomputer keeps the last humans alive only to torture them, turning its own consciousness into an eternal machine grievance.

records.quote: Computers and the programs will start thinking and the people will stop.
records.notes: A concise early-computing warning line that reads clearly and expands the machine-dependency lane.

review.decision_note: Fresh candidate from the early-computing canon lane.

source_work.title: TRON
source_work.creator: Steven Lisberger
source_work.summary: A programmer is pulled into a digital world where programs fight under authoritarian control and humans begin surrendering more thought to machines.

records.quote: Welcome to the desert of the real.
records.notes: A compact Matrix line with huge recognition that can widen the machine-simulation lane.

review.decision_note: Fresh candidate from the Matrix follow-up lane.

source_work.title: The Matrix
source_work.creator: The Wachowskis
source_work.summary: Neo discovers that apparent reality is a machine-run illusion and awakens into a devastated world built on human harvesting.
